generatedSentence: Our protagonist, a renowned wanderer and slacker, often found himself on a sofa, sketching artwork with an airbrush, dreaming of a wonderland. His wardrobe was minimal, his inventory mostly leftover oils and old literature. He'd briefly perform on the street as an offbeat drummer, his drums sometimes sounding improperly tuned. He hankers for adventure, an escape from his bungalow and the gridlock that often choked the nearby street. He’d kickstart his old engine, knowing it would sometimes backfire, but it was his only way to explore the width of the world before he became completely oblivious to its beauty. His eyelash would occasionally twitch, betraying a deeper desire. A garish celebrity, a blonde lawmaker with a tongue for fiction and a penchant for mischief, planned to hijack the longest airline route, turning the airfield into a scene of potential killings. Her gormless protector, a widowed racer, was unopposed in his task. He'd practice his workout routines, fueled by bacons and garlic, maintaining an untouched facade. The plan involved disabling the gearbox and making the plane slide into a controlled implosion, creating a picture of chaos. The freight bay, full of teacups and an ancient utensil, was the target. This act of heresy would lead to international withdrawal, creating a chalky future. Our wanderer, now an international racer, discovered the plot through guesswork and an occasional message from a network of phantoms. He saw the obstructive gates of the private airfield, noticing the garish rims of the getaway vehicle. He knew he had to perform quickly. He found the keypad on the plane's engine panel, which the raider had left imperfectly secured. He engaged in a frantic scrimmage, almost knotting his underwear in the process, to prevent the firebomb from igniting. His actions were elevated by sheer will. He felt like a mayfly against a horse, battling backwards against a tide of destruction. He activated his jetpacks, leaving the bungalow behind. Ultimately, the hero's honest understanding and multitasking brilliance, enhanced by a touch of alchemy, created a magnetic field, filling the environment with a protective force. He watched the asteroids harmlessly deflect, a lyrical goodbye to the destruction that might have been. The automatic system he deployed maintained peace, and he savored a leftover chamomile tea. The otter, his quiet companion, watched as the last remnants of oil were contained. No more romancing with danger, just a calm getaway, proving that even a slacker can be a protector.
panelSentences: Our protagonist, a renowned wanderer and slacker, often found himself on a sofa, sketching artwork with an airbrush, dreaming of a wonderland. His wardrobe was minimal, his inventory mostly leftover oils and old literature. He'd briefly perform on the street as an offbeat drummer, his drums sometimes sounding improperly tuned. He hankers for adventure, an escape from his bungalow and the gridlock that often choked the nearby street. He’d kickstart his old engine, knowing it would sometimes backfire, but it was his only way to explore the width of the world before he became completely oblivious to its beauty. His eyelash would occasionally twitch, betraying a deeper desire.

A garish celebrity, a blonde lawmaker with a tongue for fiction and a penchant for mischief, planned to hijack the longest airline route, turning the airfield into a scene of potential killings. Her gormless protector, a widowed racer, was unopposed in his task. He'd practice his workout routines, fueled by bacons and garlic, maintaining an untouched facade. The plan involved disabling the gearbox and making the plane slide into a controlled implosion, creating a picture of chaos. The freight bay, full of teacups and an ancient utensil, was the target. This act of heresy would lead to international withdrawal, creating a chalky future.

Our wanderer, now an international racer, discovered the plot through guesswork and an occasional message from a network of phantoms. He saw the obstructive gates of the private airfield, noticing the garish rims of the getaway vehicle. He knew he had to perform quickly. He found the keypad on the plane's engine panel, which the raider had left imperfectly secured. He engaged in a frantic scrimmage, almost knotting his underwear in the process, to prevent the firebomb from igniting. His actions were elevated by sheer will. He felt like a mayfly against a horse, battling backwards against a tide of destruction. He activated his jetpacks, leaving the bungalow behind.

Ultimately, the hero's honest understanding and multitasking brilliance, enhanced by a touch of alchemy, created a magnetic field, filling the environment with a protective force. He watched the asteroids harmlessly deflect, a lyrical goodbye to the destruction that might have been. The automatic system he deployed maintained peace, and he savored a leftover chamomile tea. The otter, his quiet companion, watched as the last remnants of oil were contained. No more romancing with danger, just a calm getaway, proving that even a slacker can be a protector.
rawPanelSentences: Our protagonist, a renowned wanderer and slacker, often found himself on a sofa, sketching artwork with an airbrush, dreaming of a wonderland. His wardrobe was minimal, his inventory mostly leftover oils and old literature. He'd briefly perform on the street as an offbeat drummer, his drums sometimes sounding improperly tuned. He hankers for adventure, an escape from his bungalow and the gridlock that often choked the nearby street. He’d kickstart his old engine, knowing it would sometimes backfire, but it was his only way to explore the width of the world before he became completely oblivious to its beauty. His eyelash would occasionally twitch, betraying a deeper desire.

A garish celebrity, a blonde lawmaker with a tongue for fiction and a penchant for mischief, planned to hijack the longest airline route, turning the airfield into a scene of potential killings. Her gormless protector, a widowed racer, was unopposed in his task. He'd practice his workout routines, fueled by bacons and garlic, maintaining an untouched facade. The plan involved disabling the gearbox and making the plane slide into a controlled implosion, creating a picture of chaos. The freight bay, full of teacups and an ancient utensil, was the target. This act of heresy would lead to international withdrawal, creating a chalky future.

Our wanderer, now an international racer, discovered the plot through guesswork and an occasional message from a network of phantoms. He saw the obstructive gates of the private airfield, noticing the garish rims of the getaway vehicle. He knew he had to perform quickly. He found the keypad on the plane's engine panel, which the raider had left imperfectly secured. He engaged in a frantic scrimmage, almost knotting his underwear in the process, to prevent the firebomb from igniting. His actions were elevated by sheer will. He felt like a mayfly against a horse, battling backwards against a tide of destruction. He activated his jetpacks, leaving the bungalow behind.

Ultimately, the hero's honest understanding and multitasking brilliance, enhanced by a touch of alchemy, created a magnetic field, filling the environment with a protective force. He watched the asteroids harmlessly deflect, a lyrical goodbye to the destruction that might have been. The automatic system he deployed maintained peace, and he savored a leftover chamomile tea. The otter, his quiet companion, watched as the last remnants of oil were contained. No more romancing with danger, just a calm getaway, proving that even a slacker can be a protector.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting, thick white gutters, clean black borders. Protagonist: wearing a lime-green flight suit and a silver chrome helmet. TOP-LEFT PANEL: Protagonist on a futuristic sofa, sketching on a glowing tablet, a wide street visible through a window, wide shot. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: A blonde lawmaker with a mischievous grin, standing before a control panel, looking over her shoulder at a gormless protector, medium shot. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: Protagonist in a thrilling jetpack chase through an asteroid field towards a futuristic airplane, dynamic action shot, low angle. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: Protagonist, calm and resolute, petting an otter, with a tranquil, protected cityscape in the background, close-up.

definitions.definition: A miniature, handheld paint sprayer, powered by compressed air or other gas, used for delicate, artistic painting, or sometimes retouching of photographs.
To paint using an airbrush.
To touch up or enhance a photograph or person, often with intent to mislead.
A large construction used in warfare, such as a battering ram, catapult etc.
A tool; a utensil or implement.
A complex mechanical device which converts energy into useful motion or physical effects.
A person or group of people which influence a larger group; a driving force.
The part of a car or other vehicle which provides the force for motion, now especially one powered by internal combustion.
A self-powered vehicle, especially a locomotive, used for pulling cars along a track.
A software or hardware system responsible for a specific technical task (usually with qualifying word).
definitions.example: a graphics engine

definitions.definition: Ingenuity; cunning, trickery, guile.
The result of cunning; something ingenious, a contrivance; (in negative senses) a plot, a scheme.
Natural talent; genius.
Anything used to effect a purpose; any device or contrivance; an agent.
To equip with an engine; said especially of steam vessels.
definitions.example: Vessels are often built by one firm and engined by another.

definitions.definition: To assault with an engine.
To contrive; to put into action.
To rack; to torture.
One of the hairs which grow along the edge of eyelids
A paved part of road, usually in a village or a town.
definitions.example: Walk down the street until you see a hotel on the right.

definitions.definition: A road as above but including the sidewalks (pavements) and buildings.
definitions.example: I live on the street down from Joyce Avenue.

definitions.definition: The people who live in such a road, as a neighborhood.
The people who spend a great deal of time on the street in urban areas, especially, the young, the poor, the unemployed, and those engaged in illegal activities.
An illicit or contraband source, especially of drugs.
definitions.example: I got some pot cheap on the street.

definitions.definition: Streetwise slang.
A great distance.
definitions.example: He's streets ahead of his sister in all the subjects in school.

definitions.definition: Each of the three opportunities that players have to bet, after the flop, turn and river.
Living in the streets.
definitions.example: a street cat; a street urchin

definitions.definition: (urban toponymy) By restriction, the streets that run perpendicular to avenues.
To build or equip with streets.
To eject; to throw onto the streets.
(by extension) To heavily defeat.
To go on sale.
To proselytize in public.
Having street cred; conforming to modern urban trends.

The flexible muscular organ in the mouth that is used to move food around, for tasting and that is moved into various positions to modify the flow of air from the lungs in order to produce different sounds in speech.
This organ, as taken from animals used for food (especially cows).−
definitions.example: cold tongue with mustard

definitions.definition: (metonym) A language.
definitions.example: He was speaking in his native tongue.

definitions.definition: Speakers of a language, collectively.
Voice (the distinctive sound of a person's speech); accent (distinctive manner of pronouncing a language).
Manner of speaking, often habitually.
(metonym) A person speaking in a specified manner (most often plural).
The power of articulate utterance; speech generally.
Discourse; fluency of speech or expression.
Discourse; fluency of speech or expression.
Honourable discourse; eulogy.
(often in the plural) Glossolalia.
In a shoe, the flap of material that goes between the laces and the foot (so called because it resembles a tongue in the mouth).
Any large or long physical protrusion on an automotive or machine part or any other part that fits into a long groove on another part.
A projection, or slender appendage or fixture.
A long, narrow strip of land, projecting from the mainland into a sea or lake.
The pole of a vehicle; especially, the pole of an ox cart, to the end of which the oxen are yoked.
The clapper of a bell.
An individual point of flame from a fire.
A small sole (type of fish).
A short piece of rope spliced into the upper part of standing backstays, etc.; also, the upper main piece of a mast composed of several pieces.
A reed.
A division of formation; A layer or member of a formation that pinches out in one direction.
On a wind instrument, to articulate a note by starting the air with a tap of the tongue, as though by speaking a 'd' or 't' sound (alveolar plosive).
definitions.example: Playing wind instruments involves tonguing on the reed or mouthpiece.

definitions.definition: To manipulate with the tongue, as in kissing or oral sex.
To protrude in relatively long, narrow sections.
definitions.example: a soil horizon that tongues into clay

definitions.definition: To join by means of a tongue and groove.
definitions.example: to tongue boards together

definitions.definition: To talk; to prate.
To speak; to utter.
To chide; to scold.
A small, controlled fire set in the path of a larger uncontrolled fire, in order to limit the spread of the large fire by removing its fuel.

An explosion produced either by a running internal combustion engine that occurs in the air intake or exhaust system rather than inside the combustion chamber or unburned fuel or hydrocarbons ignited somewhere in the exhaust system.
A premature explosion in the cylinder of a gas or oil engine during the exhaust or the compression stroke, tending to drive the piston in the wrong direction.
An explosion in the exhaust passages of an internal combustion engine.
(of a gun, cannon, Bunsen burner, etc.) To fire in the opposite direction, for example due to an obstruction in the barrel.
(of an engine) To experience a premature ignition of fuel or an ignition of exhaust gases, making a popping sound.
To fail in a manner that brings down further misfortune.
definitions.example: His attempt to make money by importing luxury cars backfired horribly when fuel prices tripled.

definitions.definition: At an earlier time.
definitions.example: I've never done this before.

definitions.definition: In advance.
At the front end.
Earlier than (in time).
definitions.example: I want this done before Monday.

definitions.definition: In front of in space.
definitions.example: He stood before me.

definitions.definition: In the presence of.
definitions.example: He performed before the troops in North Africa.

definitions.definition: Under consideration, judgment, authority of (someone).

definitions.definition: In store for, in the future of (someone).
In front of, according to a formal system of ordering items.

definitions.definition: At a higher or greater position than, in a ranking.
definitions.example: An entrepreneur puts market share and profit before quality, an amateur intrinsic qualities before economical considerations.

definitions.definition: In advance of the time when.
Rather or sooner than.
definitions.example: I'll die before I'll tell you anything about it.

definitions.definition: A pale yellowish (golden brown) color, especially said of hair color.
A person with this hair color.
Of a bleached or pale golden (light yellowish) colour.

definitions.definition: (of a person) Having blond hair.
(especially of a woman) Stupid, ignorant, naive.
An instance of hijacking; the illegal seizure of a vehicle; a hijacking.
An instance of a seizure and redirection of a process.

Preflop, the position two before the dealer.
To forcibly stop and seize control of some vehicle in order to rob it or to reach a destination (especially an airplane, truck or a boat).
To seize control of some process or resource to achieve a purpose other than its originally intended one.
To seize control of a networked computer by means of infecting it with a worm or other malware, thereby turning it into a zombie.
To change software settings without a user's knowledge so as to force that user to visit a certain web site (to hijack a browser).

To take a long position in.
Having much distance from one terminating point on an object or an area to another terminating point (usually applies to horizontal dimensions; see Usage Notes below).

definitions.definition: Having great duration.

definitions.definition: Seemingly lasting a lot of time, because it is boring or tedious or tiring.
Not short; tall.
Possessing or owning stocks, bonds, commodities or other financial instruments with the aim of benefiting of the expected rise in their value.

definitions.definition: Of a fielding position, close to the boundary (or closer to the boundary than the equivalent short position).
(of a ball or a shot) Landing beyond the baseline, and therefore deemed to be out.

definitions.definition: Occurring or coming after an extended interval; distant in time; far away.
Over a great distance in space.

definitions.definition: For a particular duration.
definitions.example: How long is it until the next bus arrives?

definitions.definition: For a long duration.

definitions.definition: To await, aspire, desire greatly (something to occur or to be true)

definitions.definition: To be appropriate to, to pertain or belong to.
To belong.
The body of all written works.
The collected creative writing of a nation, people, group or culture.

Written fiction of a high standard.
definitions.example: However, even “literary” science fiction rarely qualifies as literature, because it treats characters as sets of traits rather than as fully realized human beings with unique life stories. —Adam Cadre, 2008

definitions.definition: A doorlike structure outside a house.
Doorway, opening, or passage in a fence or wall.
Movable barrier.

definitions.definition: The amount of money made by selling tickets to a concert or a sports event.

To furnish with a gate.

A journey.

An open field designated for the taking off and landing of aircraft, but which, unlike an airport, does not necessarily have terminals or paved runways.
To form into a knot; to tie with a knot or knots.

The formation of a knot.
The swelling of the bulbus glandis.
(operations) The stock of an item on hand at a particular location or business.

definitions.example: The main job of the night shift was to inventory the store, and restock when necessary.

definitions.definition: Famous, celebrated, or well-known.

definitions.definition: A raised area of a building's floor, usually covered with carpeting, used for sitting.

To furnish with one or more sofas.
To seat or lay down on a sofa.
(manner) In a brief manner, summarily.

definitions.definition: (duration) For a brief period.

definitions.definition: To be brief, in short.

definitions.definition: To do something; to execute.

definitions.definition: To do (something) in front of an audience, such as acting or music, often in order to entertain.

definitions.definition: A representation of anything (as a person, a landscape, a building) upon canvas, paper, or other surface, by drawing, painting, printing, photography, etc.

A painting.

definitions.definition: A photograph.

definitions.definition: A motion picture.

definitions.definition: (in the plural) ("the pictures") Cinema (as a form of entertainment).

definitions.definition: An attractive sight.

definitions.definition: The art of painting; representation by painting.
A figure; a model.
Situation.

definitions.definition: To represent in or with a picture.
To imagine or envision.
To depict or describe vividly.
Something left behind; an excess or remainder.

definitions.definition: (chiefly in the plural, usually of food) Remaining after a meal is complete or eaten for a later meal or snack.

definitions.definition: Remaining; left behind; extra; in reserve.

Petroleum-based liquid used as fuel or lubricant.
An oil painting.
Oil paint.

definitions.definition: The beats not normally accented in a measure.

definitions.example: He has such an offbeat sense of humor that hardly anyone finds his jokes amusing.

Travelling salesman
A drumstick (the lower part of a chicken or turkey leg).
Any of various fish of the family Kyphosidae which make a drumming sound.

The wandering albatross, Diomedea exulans.
The state of being wide.
The measurement of the extent of something from side to side.
A piece of material measured along its smaller dimension, especially fabric.
The horizontal distance between a batsman and the ball as it passes him.
The use of all the width of the pitch, from one side to the other.

definitions.definition: A company that flies airplanes to transport people and goods.
An instance of someone being killed.
(usually as make a killing) A large amount of money.

The action of imploding.
The act or action of bringing to or as if to a center.

A wheelrim.

To form a rim on.

definitions.definition: (of a ball) To roll around a rim.

To lick the anus of a partner as a sexual act.
Any of the many fragile insects of the order Ephemeroptera that develop in fresh water and live very briefly as winged adults.
Oriented toward the back.

definitions.definition: Reversed.

definitions.definition: Behind current trends or technology.
definitions.example: Modern medicine regards the use of leeches as a backwards practice.

definitions.definition: Clumsy, inept, or inefficient, especially in learning.
definitions.example: He was a very backwards scholar, but he was a marvel on the football field.

definitions.definition: A piece of equipment that may be strapped to a person’s back and generate vertical thrust by a downward discharge of jet fluid, thereby rendering the person capable of flight.
To crave, want or desire.

definitions.definition: In a manner that is pleasing to hear, as singing or speaking; in a lyrical manner.
In terms of the lyrics.

Underpants (boxers, briefs, panties, etc) and often bras.

Petroleum-based liquid used as fuel or lubricant.
An oil painting.
Oil paint.

To grease with oil for cooking.

Any similar hollow, cylindrical object.
definitions.example: Replace the drum unit of your printer.

definitions.definition: A barrel or large cylindrical container for liquid transport and storage.

A social gathering or assembly held in the evening.

definitions.definition: To promote (someone) to a higher rank.
To confer honor or nobility on (someone).

definitions.example: A talented chef can elevate everyday ingredients into gourmet delights.

To increase the intensity or degree of (something).

definitions.definition: To lift the spirits of (someone)
To intoxicate in a slight degree; to make (someone) tipsy.
(Latinism) To attempt to make (something) seem less important, remarkable, etc.
An elevated railway.
Raised, particularly above ground level.
Increased, particularly above a normal level.

definitions.definition: Of a higher rank or status.
Running with administration rights granted
definitions.example: Install all the required tools from an elevated console.

definitions.definition: Intoxicated; drunk
One who procrastinates or is lazy.
A person lacking a sense of direction in life; an underachiever.
A person who seeks to avoid military service.
A user of the Slackware Linux operating system.
A member of a certain 1990s subculture associated with Generation X.
To occupy fully, to take up all of.
To add contents to (a container, cavity or the like) so that it is full.
To enter (something), making it full.
To become full.
definitions.example: the bucket filled with rain;  the sails fill with wind

definitions.definition: To become pervaded with something.
definitions.example: My heart filled with joy.

definitions.definition: To satisfy or obey (an order, request or requirement).
definitions.example: The pharmacist filled my prescription for penicillin.

definitions.definition: To install someone, or be installed, in (a position or office), eliminating a vacancy.
definitions.example: Sorry, no more applicants. The position has been filled.

definitions.definition: To treat (a tooth) by adding a dental filling to it.
definitions.example: Dr. Smith filled Jim's cavity with silver amalgam.

definitions.definition: To fill or supply fully with food; to feed; to satisfy.
To trim (a yard) so that the wind blows on the after side of the sails.
(of a male) To have sexual intercourse with (a female).
definitions.example: Did you fill that girl last night?

definitions.definition: Anything that is used to fill something.
The contents of a pie, etc.
Any material used to fill a cavity in a tooth or the result of using such material.
definitions.example: I will be using a rapid-setting cement filling.

definitions.definition: The woof in woven fabrics.
Prepared wort added to ale to cleanse it.
A religious experience attributed to the Holy Ghost "filling" a believer.
Of food, that satisfies the appetite by filling the stomach.

definitions.definition: A doctrine held by a member of a religion at variance with established religious beliefs, especially dissension from Roman Catholic dogma.
A controversial or unorthodox opinion held by a member of a group, as in politics, philosophy or science.
A condition of total, interlocking traffic congestion on the streets or highways of a crowded city, in which no one can move because everyone is in someone else's way.

On a smaller scale: the situation in which cars enter a signal-controlled intersection too late during the green light cycle, and are unable to clear the intersection (due to congestion in the next block) when the light turns red, thus blocking the cross traffic when it's their turn to go. Repeated at enough intersections, this phenomenon can lead to citywide gridlock.
(by extension) any paralysis of a complex system due to severe congestion, conflict, or deadlock.
To cause traffic congestion
With no or little opposition
Without an opponent.
Composite plant with a fragrance reminiscent of apples:
Any of several other similar plants. (See below)
Short for camomile tea.
(usually followed by to or of) Lacking awareness; unmindful; unaware, unconscious of.
Failing to remember; forgetful.
The ancient search for a universal panacea, and of the philosopher's stone, that eventually developed into chemistry.
The causing of any sort of mysterious sudden transmutation.
Any elaborate transformation process or algorithm.
Someone who has represented their country in a particular sport.
definitions.example: The United team includes five England internationals.

definitions.definition: A game or contest between two or more nations.
A transnational organization of political parties of similar ideology.
Of or having to do with more than one nation.
Between or among nations
definitions.example: an international discussion

definitions.definition: Participated in by two or more nations
definitions.example: an international competition

definitions.definition: Common to, or affecting, two or more nations.
definitions.example: an international rule

definitions.definition: Serving two or more nations
definitions.example: an international airport

definitions.definition: Of or concerning the association called the International.
Independent of national boundaries; common to all people.
definitions.example: The atmosphere is an international resource.

definitions.definition: Foreign; of another nation.
definitions.example: an international student

definitions.definition: In an imperfect manner or degree; not fully or completely.
A piece of material that attracts some metals by magnetism.
(preceded by a noun) A person or thing that attracts what is denoted by the preceding noun.
definitions.example: He always had a girl on his arm – he's a bit of a babe magnet.

definitions.definition: Cured meat from the sides, belly or back of a pig.
Thin slices of the above in long strips.
The police or spies.
definitions.example: Run! It's the bacon!

definitions.definition: Road rash.
A saucisse.
Any of several animals related to Equus ferus caballus.
Equipment with legs.
Type of equipment.

A mass of earthy matter, or rock of the same character as the wall rock, occurring in the course of a vein, as of coal or ore; hence, to take horse (said of a vein) is to divide into branches for a distance.
The sedative, antidepressant, and anxiolytic drug morphine, chiefly when used illicitly.

An informal variant of basketball in which players match shots made by their opponent(s), each miss adding a letter to the word "horse", with 5 misses spelling the whole word and eliminating a player, until only the winner is left. Also HORSE, H-O-R-S-E or H.O.R.S.E. (see Variations of basketball#H-O-R-S-E).
(among students) A translation or other illegitimate aid in study or examination.
(among students) horseplay; tomfoolery
An utterance of goodbye, the wishing of farewell to someone.
definitions.example: They made their good-byes.

definitions.definition: To say goodbye; to wish somebody farewell on parting.
Farewell; a formula used to another person or persons when the speaker, writer, or person addressed is departing.
An aquatic or marine carnivorous mammal in the subfamily Lutrinae of the family Mustelidae, which also includes weasels, polecats, badgers, and others.
A hairy man with a slender physique, in contrast with a bear, who is more thickset.
Any member of the taxonomic class Asteroidea; a starfish
A naturally occurring solid object, which is smaller than a planet and is not a comet, that orbits a star
In the Solar system, such a body that orbits within the orbit of Jupiter
Indicating the last item.
definitions.example: Firstly,… Secondly,… Ultimately,…

definitions.definition: Indicating the most important action.
synonyms: at the end of the day
when all is said and done
definitions.example: Ultimately, he will have to make a decision before the end of the week.

definitions.definition: Used to indicate the etymon at which a given etymological derivation terminates.
definitions.example: Cognate to Gottscheerish boßər, bàsser. Ultimately cognate to standard High German Wasser.

synonyms: at the end of the day
when all is said and done

definitions.definition: A person who does something only occasionally.
Occurring or appearing irregularly from time to time, but not often.
definitions.example: He took an occasional glass of wine.

definitions.definition: Created for a specific occasion.
definitions.example: Elgar's music was not created to be occasional music for high-school graduations.

definitions.definition: Intended for use as the occasion requires.
definitions.example: What your living room needs are some occasional chairs.

definitions.definition: Acting in the indicated role from time to time.
definitions.example: He is an occasional writer of letters to the editor.

definitions.definition: To support (someone), to back up or assist (someone) in an action.
To keep up; to preserve; to uphold (a state, condition etc.).
To declare or affirm (a clause) to be true; to assert.
The surroundings of, and influences on, a particular item of interest.
The natural world or ecosystem.
All the elements that affect a system or its inputs and outputs.
A particular political or social setting, arena or condition.
The software and/or hardware existing on any particular computer system.

definitions.definition: The environment of a function at a point during the execution of a program is the set of identifiers in the function's scope and their bindings at that point.
The set of variables and their values in a namespace that an operating system associates with a process.
Remaining in its original, pristine state, undamaged; not altered.

definitions.definition: Not eaten.

definitions.definition: Not influenced, affected or swayed.

definitions.definition: Not having come in contact.

definitions.definition: Not talked about.

definitions.definition: A car with automatic transmission.

definitions.definition: A semi-automatic pistol.

definitions.definition: Capable of operating without external control or intervention.
definitions.example: The automatic clothes washer was a great labor-saving device.

definitions.definition: Done out of habit or without conscious thought.
definitions.example: Absent-minded doodling is a form of automatic art.

definitions.definition: Necessary, inevitable, prescribed by logic, law, etc.
definitions.example: Spitting at another player means an automatic red card.

definitions.definition: (of a firearm such as a machine gun) Firing continuously as long as the trigger is pressed until ammunition is exhausted.
definitions.example: Fully automatic weapons cannot be legally owned by private citizens in the US, except in very special circumstances, as by private security companies.

definitions.definition: (of a handgun) An autoloader; a semi-automatic or self-loading pistol, as opposed to a revolver or other manually actuated handgun, which fires one shot per pull of the trigger; distinct from machine guns.
definitions.example: The US Army adopted John Browning's M1911 pistol as its sidearm, chambered in .45 ACP (Automatic Colt Pistol).

definitions.definition: (of a local variable) Automatically added to and removed from the stack during the course of function calls.
(of a group) Having one or more finite-state automata
A metal bar on a motorcycle used to start its engine.
An act of starting a motorcycle by quickly depressing the kick start with one's foot.

definitions.definition: A fast or strong start; help with starting or beginning well.

definitions.definition: An impetus that starts or restarts some process.
To start a motorcycle.

definitions.definition: To start or begin strongly or quickly.

definitions.definition: To make (something) active, functional, or productive again; to reinvigorate (an activity, system, or process).

A racehorse.

Consisting of or containing chalk.

Receiving from someone's care what one has earlier entrusted to them. Usually refers to money.

The sum of money taken from a bank account.

Anything that similarly stores or houses something.
The contents of a wardrobe: an individual's entire collection of clothing.

Any collection of anything.

Badger feces, particularly used in tracking game.
To act as a wardrobe department, to provide clothing or sets of clothes.

Conduct that playfully causes petty annoyance.

Something apparently seen, heard, or sensed, but having no physical reality; an image that appears only in the mind; an illusion or delusion.

Characterized by sweatshop conditions.

Literary type using invented or imaginative writing, instead of real facts, usually written as prose.

To adorn or grace; to honour; to make becoming, appropriate, or honourable.

Honestly; really.

A mechanism consisting of a part which slides on or against a guide.

A valve that works by sliding, such as in a trombone.
A transparent plate bearing an image to be projected to a screen.

A lively dance from County Kerry, in 12/8 time.
A small dislocation in beds of rock along a line of fissure.

A clasp or brooch for a belt, etc.
(footwear) A shoe that is backless and open-toed.
(speech therapy) A voluntary stutter used as a technique to control stuttering in one's speech.
To (cause to) move in continuous contact with a surface

definitions.definition: To drop down and skid into a base.

A rough fight.

A unit of measure; a teacupful.

Cultural or emotional associations.
To transport (goods).

Overly ostentatious; so colourful as to be in bad taste.

Opinion, judgement or outlook.

definitions.definition: An agreement of minds; harmony; something mutually understood or agreed upon.
Sympathy.
definitions.example: He showed much understanding for my problems when he heard about my past.

definitions.definition: Showing compassion.
Knowing; skilful.
